title: Nova’s Perfected
description: SPEEDIER THAN FABULOUSLY OPTIMIZED! PLUS TERRAIN MODS. VANILLA PLUS!
body:
# **Nova's Perfected** 
## SPEEDIER THAN SODIUM PLUS OR FABULOUSLY OPTIMIZED 
## AVAILABLE FOR 1.21.4, 1.21.1 AND 1.8.9(more focused on pvp)!!!!
### Try it and you wont regret it!
**Join my [Discord](https://discord.gg/bYNCcn2v)!**


![FPS](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/057188672d0a8707c9345d570f267ae61443025d_0.webp)

### REACHED **1100 FPS** on max settings, M2 macbook AIR not plugged in to charger.

### REACHED 70 FPS on MAX SHADERS(Complimentary), same specs.

### _**TONS** OF TERRAIN GENERATION AND STRUCTURE MODS WITHOUT SACRIFICING **ANY** PERFORMANCE!!!_

### This **MAY** look like a lot of mods but TRUST me, it wont dissapoint.

### Aiming to bring the **BEST** performance while experiencing the pinnacle of vanilla+ Minecraft.

([download my hotbar.nbt](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1ew3zRwfY5IQMpNaC-ENyEKT8Se8wAxt0/view?usp=sharing) as a part for the [uncensored library project](https://uncensoredlibrary.com/))
load by replacing file in modrinth instance(open folder) hotbar.nbt with this one. Load by checking the keybind of loading hotbar in options and going into creative and pressing[keybind+1]

**Performance**
Nova's Perfected is faster than competing game clients, thanks to modern performance mods like Sodium, Lithium, and others. The bundled mods have been carefully selected to be safe in your worlds and provide noticeable speed benefits on any device.

**Feels familiar**
Nova's perfected includes features that allow you to experience Minecraft just as you remember it, no matter what version you've used before. This means you can use game controllers, play local worlds with friends, view advancements in a large window, and much more, embracing the VANILLA PLUS moto.

**Up to date**
Nova's Perfected receives fast updates and stands as one of the first modpacks to upgrade to newer Minecraft versions. Updating the modpack is easy and will preserve your mod settings, where possible.

**Built on Fabric**
Nova's Perfected uses the Fabric mod loader, which is lightweight, fast, and supports a lot of mods. You can use the modpack as a base to add your favorite mods to.

**Open development**
Nova's Perfected is open-source, and so are most of its mods. We welcome user suggestions and collaborate with mod developers to create a better user experience for everyone.

**Helpful community**
We have a growing community of friendly members who enjoy discussing the pack, translating its content, and helping each other. Join us today by contacting me:).

Not every resources pack is listed, and are not required. Add them at your own will:

[Fresh Animations Extensions](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/fresh-animations-extensions)

[Fresh Skeleton Physics](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/fresh-skeleton-physics)

[Human Era Villagers](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/human-era-villagers-illagers)

[Icons Branding Remover](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/icons-advertisement-removal)

[Recreated Containers](https://minecraftstorage.com/textures/recreated-containers)

[More Mob Variants X Fresh Animations](https://modrinth.com/resourcepack/more-mob-variants-fresh-animations-addon)

**PROPS TO ALL THE MOD DEVs THANK YOU :)**


<details>
<summary>Versioning</summary>
*NP is short-form of Nova's Perfected
  
The versioning format is <MC version>-x.y(.z), where
the MC version taken is the minor release the version of NP is for,
x is a mod list revision (addition/removal of a mod)
y is the config revision (incremented every time a config tweak is added or changed)
z is incremented when there's a version with only mod updates and little to no changes otherwise.
x.y.z is reset to 1.0.0 for every new MC release as different MC versions are very likely to have different mod sets available for them.

Derivative packs

You can use the configuration files from NP in your modpack as long as you make it clear in your modpack's description that you have (a note at the end is enough), preferrably with a link back to this page.


</details>



<details>
<summary>C2ME</summary>
Through tests I have discovered that it actually decreases FPS so I diddynt add it.


</details>
